level4.p: Email:

level2.p: The development of modern society complicated socio-economic conditions and, as a result, increased the instability of the economic environment. The spread of the COVID-19 pandemic made the shift to remote employment a necessity, thus the research into the issues of forming a digital strategy and digitization of personnel management has become especially relevant. The generalization of trends and the development of the most relevant personnel management models corresponding to the current conditions of uncertainty have become the goal of this study. The evaluation of trends in personnel management in conditions of uncertainty was carried out through surveys and questionnaires of companies of social direction, as well as educational institutions, public catering, retail trade, and institutions providing social services. The study involved 69 Ukrainian companies in total. The most common in Ukraine are HR strategies aimed at using the opportunities identified in the SWOT analysis. The current research shows that digitalization and gamification, lifelong learning, HR branding and HR marketing, and leadership development are the most relevant in personnel management. The strategic direction of “sustainable development thinking” includes all global trends—from psychological well-being in the workplace and work-life balance to socially responsible and environmentally friendly behavior, from the digitalization of HR management processes to an increase in the responsibility of virtual teams, and management diversification in general. Therefore, companies that apply “sustainable development thinking” in the strategy of personnel management under conditions of uncertainty can maximize their potential on the market and increase the profitability of their activities.

level3.p: Keywords

Modern economic processes develop unevenly. They combine stages of stable functioning with periods of chaotic dynamics, that is, of a nonlinear nature, which is inevitably associated with the occurrence of unpredictable situations and conditions of uncertainty. The onset of the COVID-19 pandemic brought about changes in understanding of digitalization and its impact on the society and economy. All areas of our activities like education and work, communication and entertainment, shopping, and access to social services require advanced digital technologies nowadays.

Since the beginning of 2020, measures taken by many countries to battle the COVID-19 pandemic have made the digital transformation speed much faster. Volumes of online commerce and transactions have increased. The workforce engaged in remote work has grown significantly. Digitalization benefited some areas of work like online surveys, freelancers, or delivery, and they have expanded during the pandemic. However, there were some other areas like tourism which have experienced a decline.

p.span: According to estimates by McKinsey & Co., 400–800 million people (from 15 to 30% of the global workforce) might be out of work by 2030 due to process automation and advances in artificial intelligence. Automation can be introduced into many fields of human activities. It can take over about half of all current jobs worldwide. While McKinsey data show the possibilities of automation for 30% of all tasks in approximately 60% of all professions, the European Commission sets its estimates at 50% of all jobs in the world where human labor will become minimal or not required at all [

level3.p: The world economy is characterized by radical changes caused by both a new wave of technological innovations and the evolution of society, the strengthening and diversification of peoples’ needs, and creative possibilities. These transformational processes have a systemic nature and lead to the emergence of qualitatively new structural characteristics of the global economy, which collectively mean the formation of a new technological and socio-economic stage of human life. Digitization is one of the megatrends of the globalized world. It determines the evolution of society and economy through digital transformation. The digital transformation processes take place globally, and the speed of their implementation is unequaled.

The world is constantly evolving, work tasks are becoming automated, and robots are taking people's jobs. It is these changes that present HR services with new organizational challenges, prompt unexpected decisions, and force them to work in conditions of instabilities and social upheaval in conditions of uncertainty.

p.span: The pace of the fourth industrial revolution has become faster. The world economy is now changing because of a new wave of technological innovations, the evolution of society, and the strengthening and diversification of peoples’ needs and creative possibilities. These transformational processes have a systemic nature and lead to the emergence of qualitatively new structural characteristics of the global economy, which collectively mean the formation of a new technological and socio-economic stage of human life [

]. Digitalization being one of the megatrends of the globalized world brings digital transformations and determines the evolution of the economy and society. The digital transformation processes take place all over the world, and the speed of their implementation is unequaled [

]. The fourth industrial revolution brings forward new developments in economic, social, and political structures, transformations in human resources management systems as well as ways to solve HR problems [

level3.p: Digitalization and the transition to a technocentric business entail a change in the culture and mindset of the organization.

p.span: This brings forward the idea of the development of the digital business strategy in HRM. The definition of the above can be stated as a strategy of HR operations that incorporates and implements digitalization as its tool to produce substantial value for the organization [

]. Digital transformation of human resource management is emerging out of the clarification of the role of HRM digitalization and the assessment of the benefits that it can bring to the organization [
Such changes in organizations require an introduction of horizontal and vertical integration into their production systems as a part of Industry 4.0 [
]. The production processes in such systems can be optimized or completed with minimal human intervention and, in many cases, humans could be substituted by machines completely [
It should be noted that taking into account the importance of implementing a digital HRM management strategy at all levels enables effective long-term personnel management [

The spread of remote employment initiated by the beginning of the COVID-19 pandemic made the research into problems raised by digitalization and the development of a digital strategy for personnel management especially relevant [

]. According to the Grand View Research report the global market for technical innovations in HR has reached $14 billion, and its projected growth will push it up to $30 billion by 2025. Digital technologies are changing the traditional model of personnel management and reorienting the attention of the management of organizations to the digital environment of the implementation of business processes. Traditionally, all digital strategies are focused on technologies that are necessary for changes in business models and create opportunities for the development of digital business. Defining a strategy is a key component of the transformation process and ensures that technology develops in a way that supports business goals [

]. In the conditions of uncertainty caused by the pandemic, health and safety are crucial for companies and require the development of organizational values on a long-term basis. In such conditions maintaining the work-life balance of employees must be controlled at all times [

Deloitte Global Human Capital Trends presented its research at Global Trends 2023. According to it, lifelong education, sustainable human development, leadership, and humanization of business processes became the top trends in HR in the world and Ukraine in particular [

The period of uncertainty brings with it quite a few challenges like emotional burnout, overtime work, employee engagement, and well-being. Hence, the humanization of business processes stands out as a priority. Companies need to organize an individual approach toward employees, build long-term relationships, and provide possibilities for continuous training to get a positive response from the staff [
]. However, it is the technical and material conditions of the work and not the worker’s needs that must be adjusted to make good working conditions during the humanization of labor [
]. Any business needs to learn to see the staff as not just workers for whom the necessary working conditions need to be created, but as people who have personal aspirations and motivation [

Adaptation to the new technological situation asks for a methodological approach. Businesses should be prepared to bring their resources and capabilities in line with the requirements of the workforce capable of operating in a digital environment [

The emerging digital era creates opportunities for a new type of people whose qualities make them new leaders. Digitalization changes the role of the leader and sets new skills that leaders need to have. A modern leader needs to combine humanitarian and digital skills to deal with complex problems effectively by communicating in a digital environment and bringing together geographically distant people. The capability to influence and change attitudes, discern and foster initiative, and quickly adapt to new challenges are also very important [
]. Faster and more effective communication, tight collaboration between parties, and changes in business dynamics affect leadership and its role in the era of digitalization [

Thus, new approaches to leadership require new qualities and competencies. To provide quality leadership in a constantly changing environment a person must be ready for quick decision-making in conditions of uncertainty, be open-minded, and adapt business to the new capabilities brought by new technologies. The ability of leaders to quickly adapt to new conditions is a necessary competency to succeed in the modern world. Digital competencies are becoming increasingly important for work in HR, and the need for advanced digital skills will only intensify in the future. HR management is actively using Internet tools and social media to become successful [

Versatility, innovation, and the capability to quickly react to the ever-changing global market are the qualities required to stay competitive. For the employee, it means that they need to have their training and development as an ongoing process if they want to succeed. Achieving this is possible only through continuous learning by constantly expanding knowledge and sets of skills. The skills of employees must evolve to meet current and long-term business requirements [

There appeared a lot of new learning technologies that allow combining the benefits of full-time education with that of mixed education and e-learning. One of them is gamification. By actively engaging students, it provides results that make the shortcomings of each system negligible [

The digitalization of human resources management and work processes brought about by the coronavirus infection helps organizations to achieve the ideal of sustainability, this goal necessitates the need for personnel with competencies that have the principles of sustainable development at their core [

The rapid development of Internet technologies has brought with it better data management which changed the ways human resource management processes enterprise data and resources. The data management becomes easier and more systematic [

To achieve the goals of the digital strategy of the company, HR needs to align all its functions with this strategy and become a headliner in promoting digitalization by selecting digitally skilled personnel who can contribute to the current and future needs of the company [

level3.p: The transition of the economies into the digital era is one of the biggest factors influencing the HR sphere in the future because digital technologies have huge potential for personnel management.
The generalization of trends and the development of the most relevant personnel management models corresponding to the current conditions of uncertainty have become the goal of this study.

The identification of the specifics of personnel management in conditions of uncertainty was preceded by the selection of representative groups of experts. Upper managers, like CEOs and heads of various structural divisions, of different companies have willingly agreed to participate as experts in the current research. Experts represented 69 Ukrainian companies which included educational institutions, the social services sphere, and retail and catering companies. The questionnaire for a wider range of participants in the sociological research was based on the data obtained as the result of the express survey. Two sections of express surveys were designed according to the principles of the Delphi method and were conducted (consecutively with the same set of questions in September and November 2022), which made it possible to single out the main features of personnel management among Ukrainian companies and to make a SWOT analysis of personnel management in modern conditions. All types of research (surveys and questionnaires) were carried out using Google services in the online environment and were anonymous. Sociological research was carried out in two stages: the first was in December 2022, and a repeat survey of the same survey participants was carried out in February 2023. This approach made it possible to confirm or refute the previous conclusions of experts and other survey participants, and to reveal inconsistent respondents’ answers.

p.span: Table
presents the profile of people who took part in the survey and questionnaire sorted by gender, size of the companies, positions in the companies, and type of businesses.

div.p: Table 1
Profile of the participants

p.em: Size of the company (by the number of employees)

td.p: Large companies with more than 500 employees

level3.p: The study evaluated current trends in personnel management and assessed the use of global trends for the development of a personnel management strategy in conditions of uncertainty.

level3.h3: 4 Conducting the Research and the Results

p.span: Volatility, uncertainty, complexity, and ambiguity are the phenomena that characterize the modern world [

]. The term VUCA was introduced at the end of last century to denote the rapidly changing world where the speed of implementation of the ideas means much more than the ideas themselves. In such difficult-to-predict conditions, the quickest to respond to the changing market trends companies have the winning strategy.

In conditions of uncertainty, the choice of the proper strategy for personnel management is of great importance. It has to be based not only on economic performance indicators but also on the well-being of personnel and the prospects for the development of the country in socio-economic areas. A correct strategy in the face of uncertainty should contain elements of digitalization of HR management in all business processes. A generalized SWOT analysis of the responses to the survey was carried out and presented in Table

div.p: Table 2
Generalized SWOT analysis of the effect of current conditions on the activities of companies

td.p: The development strategy of a company in conditions of uncertainty is not clearly defined;
High risks of losing qualified personnel during the war
The use of mixed working time regulations and increasing the use of flexible work schedules;
Provision of licensed software free of charge to Ukrainian companies;
Unforeseen expenses for maintaining the activities of companies

level3.p: Despite the existing threats, companies have the opportunity, thanks to the support of international partners, not to stop their activities. However, there are significant threats of burnout of employees, as well as exceeding the budget for relaxation. Experts point out gray areas in personnel management, namely the state of psychological vulnerability, anxiety, and burnout of personnel; misunderstanding due to the lack of a definite development strategy for the company; the threat of job loss for the staff, and the loss of qualified personnel for the employer.

To form a strategy for personnel management in conditions of uncertainty, it is necessary to focus on global trends, practices, and experience. The implementation of such practices will avoid mistakes and reduce the threat of loss of qualified personnel, labor efficiency, etc. Most Ukrainian companies study world practices and strive to implement them.

p.span: The current conditions of the socio-economic environment where all Ukrainian companies operate have left their mark on the choice of priority areas in personnel management. Digitalization and gamification (71.1%), lifelong learning (74.3%), HR branding, and HR marketing (51.2%) are the most relevant trends in personnel management (Fig.

div.p: A horizontal survey graph on H R management versus the percentage. The highest data is 74, 30% for lifelong learning and the lowest is 12, 60% for leadership.
Fig.1
Distribution of answers to the question “What are the main trends in personnel management in conditions of uncertainty?”

level3.p: If the company pays enough attention to the relevant areas of the development of personnel management, it can realize its potential in the market and maximize business profitability. Business owners face the dilemma of whether to invest in short-term or long-term success. Provided that the employer chooses a long-term perspective, mandatory financial infusions into the digitalization of the company's main processes and the gamification of training become a must. In addition, the training of employees should correspond to the concept of “lifelong learning.” Less expensive and faster in terms of the results obtained are such directions of business development as HR branding and HR marketing. At the same time, the successes of the company gained through HR branding and HR marketing may not be sustainable enough as compared to digitalization and training. The concept of “digitalization” refers to the increasingly widespread use of technologies and that brings changes into all areas of life and business. Digitalization now plays a huge role in the workplace and business. It has become a fundamental process of technological change that has affected every process of personnel management.

There are various digital solutions, from fully functional human capital management (HCM) systems to design for hiring and allocating personnel applicant tracking systems (ATS), to deal with HR tasks. And businesses all over the world are willingly investing in the digitalization of HRM,

The convenience and speed of processing data make candidate selection and allocation of resources more efficient. However, technological solutions become most effective when they supplement and provide tools for competent people. Such tools as chatbots and video interviews are gaining in popularity, the survey indicates that more than a third of respondents (37%) say that they choose to actively use them in their work.

At the same time, the trend of “sustainable development thinking” is understood by managers as a set of decisions that need to be implemented to achieve the well-being of staff, promote a green agenda, and implement a non-discriminatory policy in HR. However, based on the responses received (48.3% of the answers of respondents), it becomes apparent that there is a lack of understanding of this global trend among company executives and personnel managers.

p.span: A detailed review of all processes (according to trends identified by experts) shows a significant interest in digitalization. At the same time, not all HR processes are fully implemented in the digital environment (Fig.

div.p: A horizontal plots survey graph on a questionnaire for H R systems versus the percentage. The highest data is 82% for internal communications and the lowest is 22% for H R analytics.
Fig. 2
Distribution of answers to the question “Please, indicate the level of digitalization of HR processes in your company”

level3.p: The most common in the Ukrainian business environment are HR strategies directed at using the opportunities identified in the SWOT analysis. Thus, the majority of respondents noted an increase in the share of distance learning (76% of respondents’ answers). The trend of improving internal communications should be called positive (companies rarely cared about it before)—82%. It comes as a response to the challenges of uncertainty (the need for relocation, moving employees abroad, etc.). Traditionally, a bigger share of digitalization of the administration of personnel management processes remains (68% of the answers of respondents).

p.span: The real “picture” of the processes implemented in companies shows how effective the HR strategies are (Fig.

div.p: A horizontal survey graph on a questionnaire for H R systems versus the percentage. The highest data is 58, 30% for lifelong learning and the lowest is 10, 90% for H R leadership.
Fig. 3
Distribution of answers to the question “What trends in personnel management are in use in your company?”

p.span: Figure

shows that employers pay attention to the main trends of personnel management, but there is potential for their development, as the share of the implementation of modern trends is higher than that of the trends that characterize the general development of personnel management. Leadership development in modern companies is unreasonably low (10.9%). Scholars should primarily study leadership issues in the modern working environment, especially, in conjunction with virtual management and employee motivation. There are plenty of objectives for further research. Among them could be ways of strengthening an employee’s resilience against uncertainty. Some definite strategies for HR work toward strengthening of resilience of an employee in the workplace. However, future research can be directed toward lessening the stress of a person. Such research can contribute to improving the overall health and productivity of employees.

level3.p: The possibilities to continue education and acquire new skills are highly rated by people and become a major factor in favor of accepting a job offer. On the other hand, the fast advances in technologies, changes in demographics brought by growing life expectancy, development of new business models push organizations to incorporate education and training into their policies. Therefore, the companies that develop a culture of lifelong learning get an advantage in the market, and this policy becomes a trend in the world. The worker who is willing to continuously improve and broaden skills is currently in high demand.

p.span: In this context, the company needs to decide which strategy to use to create and foster a culture of continuous learning and developing new skills in organizations. Taking into account that modern ever-changing conditions radically changed the workforce, the ways of learning and development will be different from that of conventional workers to accommodate diverse needs. Some organizations select a gradual transition to “continuous education,” while others prefer personalization of training or integration of business processes and training. In any case, the companies choose only the best learning practices as the key ingredient in managing the workforce. To provide measures for the continuous training the HR needs to identify learning objectives related to the company goals, asses the capabilities of personnel and the needs of individuals, and create an annual training schedule. One of the best ways to reach these objectives is to convert professional growth and learning into interactive experiences through the introduction of gamification elements. The current research shows that companies of respondents organize training: often—47.3%, periodically—29.6%, rarely—21.5%, and only 1.6% of respondents did not have any training (Fig.

div.p: A piechart exhibits the distribution on data for no training is 1, 6%, rarely 21, 5%, often 47, 3%, and sometimes 29, 6%.
Fig. 4
Distribution of answers to the question “How often does your company conduct training events or learning?”

level3.p: A diverse workforce will understand and use the best working practices and norms necessary to perform tasks effectively when trained properly. Therefore, organizations need to conduct regular assessments of the training needs and provide necessary updates to the programs to make training better.

To sum up the result of the sociological research, it is possible to state that personnel management can be modernized and improved by the introduction of digital technologies to develop new types of leadership and solve complex problems in HR. Lifelong learning is indispensable for achieving these goals.

There is an opinion among scientists and practitioners regarding insufficient knowledge and the lack of understanding of modern trends in personnel management by Ukrainian employers. However, the current research showed that employers and managers are well aware of all important trends in personnel management. The main trends in HR management received high marks from the respondents. Ukrainian companies, today, face various problems determined, to a greater extent, by high socio-economic risks and uncertainty. Therefore, the design of a working personnel management strategy helps to eliminate problems that are not only peculiar to the current Ukrainian situation but common in the world. For businesses, uncertainty means certain difficulties in securing financial resources. Such a situation will lead to the reorientation of personnel management to less expensive activities—non-material motivation, personnel selection, career advancement, online training, and the development of corporate culture. Remote employment of the population is also a solution for businesses, as it allows them to retain highly qualified personnel not ready to live in Ukraine. From all the above and the research, it can be deduced that an increase in organizational resilience and digitalization are positively correlated. This finding is important for HR as well as for the whole organization. It shows the path to creating a long-term strategy that can account for all changes in social life and the needs of personnel. To a greater extent, digitalization and modern technologies ensure the sustainable development of an organization where the staff is involved in management processes, implements innovations in the workplace, and promotes the values of a socially responsible company. In this case, training and advanced training become not an obligation, but an internal part of the work culture, it is supported and developed by the company.

However, outdated methods and technologies of human resource management are often used in Ukrainian enterprises. The rapidly changing labor market poses new challenges to everyone who has to deal with personnel management. It opens ways for new paradigms and strategies to provide recruitment, training, motivation, adaptation, and other HR functions.

p.span: The issues of achieving the well-being of the staff remain controversial in Ukrainian companies. Unfortunately, sustainable development thinking has not become dominant in Ukrainian business although the effectiveness of such a strategic trend is obvious. As has been proven in studies, [

] an increase in uncertainty entails a significant increase in the risks of reducing the quality of the socio-psychological climate, increasing the level of dissatisfaction with work, and motivational burnout. Therefore, the solution to these problems in conditions of uncertainty requires the inclusion in the strategy of personnel management of provisions that allow the implementation of green agenda in HR, and well-being.

level3.p: New realities are emerging, and they pose real challenges for many companies. They break the usual stereotypes and demand deep changes in the HR strategy. However, such changes are necessary. They can guarantee the attraction and retention of strong, talented specialists capable of strengthening and developing the company.

This research proves that, in conditions of uncertainty, it is important to design and implement a strategy in personnel management that is built on the principle of economic and social harmonization. The basis of this principle is the dominant force of “sustainable development thinking” which includes all global trends—from psychological well-being in the workplace and work-life balance to socially responsible and environmentally friendly behavior, from the digitalization of HR management processes to an increase in the responsibility of virtual teams, and management diversification in general. The subsequent development of HR will be inextricably linked with such a trend as moral and ethical management in a circular economy, and collaborative solutions to the socio-economic problems of companies.

level2.h2: A Model for Quality Management in Industrial Products Based on Machine Learning and Image Processing Techniques

p.span: Dongari Rakesh
and
Kalyanapu Srinivas

div.p: Computer Science and Engineering (AI&ML), Vaagdevi Engineering College, Warangal, India
Computer Science and Engineering, Vaagdevi Engineering College, Warangal, India

level3.p: Dongari Rakesh
(Corresponding author)
Email:
Kalyanapu Srinivas

level4.p: Email:

level2.p: Products are typically mass produced in industries. Anytime mass production is involved, it presents a number of difficulties, including problems with efficiency, costs, and time consumption. Automation has been used by numerous industries as a solution to these issues. A system that checks the finished goods for flaws at the end of the production line is necessary to preserve the quality of the items. To deal with the aforementioned issues, the majority sectors of industries have started using automated systems employing image-based processing technique. An image-based processing system is one of the major components in industrial automation production operations which has high rate of effectiveness due to the availability of modernized digital cameras and various communication interfaces. Manufacturers place the highest attention on ensuring quality. Component identification during manufacturing flow must be possible with machines that can use computer vision effectively. In this paper, we present an industrial image-based processing quality management system.

level3.p: Keywords

Technology has grown significantly over time making to rely on situations where our interaction with information technology (IT) has made crucial. As a result, a lot of importance has taken on the way we are engaged with technical world. Once independent of one another, computer systems are now purposefully made to interact with one another and with people. Gadgets are being networked all over the place and for a wide variety of purposes. The majority of these gadgets include sensors that gather information and allow gadgets to communicate with one another. The acquired information may then be made available to individuals and other devices as needed. In recent years, industrial vision systems have already been the subject of extensive research. In comparison to earlier machine vision systems, the inspection systems employed by industries today for machine vision have improved efficiency and reliability. Industrial vision systems that are quicker, more cost-effective, and efficient are being developed by researchers. The operational quality, structural quality, surface quality, and dimension quality can all be inspected using industrial vision systems. Now, the methodologies employed during the various phases for inspection bring a change depending on these factors. Therefore, we focus on the quality in point of structural or assembling of the objects relevant to industry products.

p.span: Research has been done on machine vision systems for inspecting the structural quality of a device and to contribute an automatic system in the sectors of electronics and semiconductors. Several approaches and algorithms were discussed for auto-visual inspection on boards of printed circuits by Moganti et al. [
] (PCBs). A template-based matching system by Kim et al. [
] presented for spotting errors on car automatically. Chung et al. [
] later enhanced the technology to allow for the assessment of the car doors’ structure. The literature provided methods for automating railway line inspection [

]. Automated inspection systems frequently use industrial image processing. Image processing techniques are used to enhance desirable features, enhance the image quality, and more. The improved photos can be analysed to identify patterns. Finding patterns and similarities in photographs that aid in categorizing the items or elements present in the image is the goal of pattern recognition. The structural defects in an object are identified by using these methods with machine learning approaches. Recently, fuzzy logic, evolutionary algorithms, and neural networks (NNs) [

] have been applied as analysis tools (GAs). Machine learning methods are now often utilized in inspection systems. This methodology outperforms the previously well-liked template matching and design rule verification [

] methods. Machine learning-based visual inspection systems often employ supervised learning techniques including decision trees, statistical classifiers, artificial neural networks, and support vector machines.
In the past, MI learning has been applied to a variety of tasks, including image mining, object identification, target reaching, and image classification [
]. A system named for inspection using visualization technique is modelled as a Machine instance learning issue in Paul et al. study’s [

]. In this, as an example concentrate on quality control in CPU production process. CPU comprising of a variety of parts that could be lost or misplaced during the construction of CPUs as a result of specific mistakes. A system should be existed and should be placed at the end during production in order to guarantee the quality of various parts of CPU.
Smart networked systems and societies refer to various sensors, actuators along with components that are interconnected and coupled to form a massive network (SNSS) [

]. Mobile devices may also be included in the gadgets, which are not limited to stationary ones only. SNSS creates cyber-physical systems by connecting the real and virtual worlds (CPSs). The basic goal of CPS is to use sensors and actuators to connect people with the cyber environment. Through the use of CPS, a vast, incomprehensible amount of information is exchanged between various devices. In this article, we employ an architecture that combines cloud computing and CPS.
All of the system’s resources must be managed by the CPCS. It has CPUs, data services, sensors, and actuators. Cloud servers [

] must be used for every process. According to their needs, the servers at the cloud distribute the resources like required bandwidth, storage space, and energy to be consumed. The extremely sophisticated CPCS system controls, tracks, and distributes information in the real world. A task as difficult as managing and optimizing such a complex system. It’s important to run the entire infrastructure efficiently. Due to the sheer number of organizations and devices connected to this, even a tiny inaccuracy will have a significant impact on the entire system. Through the use of sensors, actuators, and processors, CPSS can help industry control the intricate industrial processes [

level3.p: This article’s remaining sections are organized as follows. The research that has been conducted in the field is provided in the section titled “Related Work”, along with various methodologies or techniques. We go over the suggested industrial image processing-based system in section “Our Work”. In the section under “Working Process”, the findings and analysis of the experiments are provided. The “Conclusion” section wraps up this article.

In this article, it is proposed to create a system that, at the end of the productions should ensure quality, effectiveness, and minimal time consumption. Machine Instance learning algorithm that can be applied here. Also, there must be a capable method for all the connected devices to effectively communicate with one another.

p.span: The algorithm proposed is used for object detection and consists of several steps. Here are the steps:
: The first step is to prepare the training data. The images are resized and cropped to different scales, and then the regions of interest (ROIs) are identified using selective search algorithm.
: Once the ROIs are identified, the second step is to extract the features of these regions using a pre-trained convolutional neural network (CNN) on the Image dataset.

: The features extracted in step 2 are fed into a support vector machine (SVM) classifier that identifies the object class of each ROI. The SVM is trained using the features extracted from the pre-trained CNN.
: Finally, the ROIs that are classified as objects are passed to a bounding box regressor that predicts the coordinates of the object in the image

div.p: The identified object coordinates are then used to search and compare in the image dataset. If it is found with no errors then the output object is successful without damage or repair, else the object at its ROI may have some manufacturing issues and is sent for reconstruction.

The identified object coordinates are then used to search and compare in the image dataset. If it is found no errors then the output object is successful without damage or repair, else the object at its ROI may have some manufacturing issues and is sent for reconstruction.

Numerous industries are turning towards automation as a solution. The automation process has to verify the quality of the product before it comes into reality. Quality of the product depends on various components of the product and their properties. If the quality is preserved before the product gets manufactured then time, efforts, and cost are saved. Therefore, our approach is an automatic system checks the finished goods for flaws at the end of the production, i.e., before the product gets into reality in order to preserve the quality (Fig.

div.p: A workflow defines how the input images are preprocessed and feature extracted through object identification and classification to predict the coordinates of each object and check if defective. If yes, go to the reconstruction phase and exit. If no, output success and exit.
Fig. 1
Work flow of the proposal

level3.p: In this proposal, we have come up with an automatic system that assesses the quality of products manufactured in industry. This can be achieved by arrangement of image-based processing systems in industries which performs analysis on the images of products taken during production for flaws or defects. The outcome is then forwarded to the concerned authority. Our work is limited to the identification of defects in the products at the production line but needs to evaluate considering various parameters at various stages the product undergoes. In future, we need to concentrate on other components like security and database management of the system which increases complex challenges.

level2.h2: An Extensive Study on Financial Fraud Detection Using Artificial Intelligence-Based Models

p.span: V. Rama Krishna
and
Sekharbabu Boddu

div.p: Department of CSE, Koneru Lakshmaiah Education Foundation, Vaddeswaram, AP, India

level3.p: V. Rama Krishna
(Corresponding author)
Email:
Sekharbabu Boddu

level4.p: Email:

level2.p: In recent years, financial fraud—defined as the fraudulent gaining of financial assets—has develop a progressively pressing issue for organizations of all stripes. Traditional techniques of fraud detection, such as manual inspections and verifications, are costly, inefficient, and prone to error. With the arrival of machine learning technologies, made possible by advancements in artificial intelligence, it is now feasible to notice fraudulent transactions by intelligently assessing a massive sum of financial data. By evaluating and synthesizing the existing literature, this work hopes to suggest an SLR that achieves this for the field of ML-based fraud finding. For this review, we active the Kitchenham technique, which involves using a set of predetermined steps to collect, analyze, and report on the most relevant articles. Using the search criteria of prominent online library databases, many reports have been created. Based on the inclusion and exclusion standards, 93 papers were chosen, synthesized, and assessed. This article covers the most common type of fraud, as well as assessment metrics and a description of popular ML algorithms for fraud detection. The bulk of documented instances of credit card theft include the usage of ANNs and SVMs, two of the most prominent ML algorithms used for fraud detection.

level3.p: Keywords

p.span: Insurance, banking, taxation, places where financial fraud is common [

]. The term “financial fraud” refers to the practice of obtaining monetary advantage using deceptive and dishonest means. Companies and sectors must now contend with a rising tide of financial fraud, including transaction fraud, money laundering, and other forms of theft. It is estimated that Americans lose $50 billion annually due to fraud. Recent studies have shown the negative effects of financial fraud on economies. Online fraud is expected to cost $25.6 billion [
] to society by 2020, up from $10.7 billion [
] in 2015, according to new study. Many years ago, numerous methods for detecting fraud were developed [

]. Most conventional approaches are manual, which is not only inefficient but also costly, inaccurate, and time-consuming. Because of this, the banking sector in particular has seen a meteoric rise in the use of automated fraud detection systems. These programs comb through mountains of information in search of red flags that could indicate fraudulent activity, such as new or unusual patterns or events [

]. There is a specific method used for investigating the possibility of fraud that is known as “fraud analysis.” Data mining, analytics, and machine learning are all utilized in this process. The efficiency of a fraud detection system is proportional to the accuracy of the information it uses and the depth of the analysis it does.

level3.p: Methods for identifying fraudulent activity and blocking their spread have advanced rapidly in recent years. However, thanks to developments in recent years, such strategies have made substantial progress.

p.span: Big data has provided a wealth of new possibilities for preventing and detecting financial fraud. Integrating enormous volumes of data from various sources (e.g., blogs) to uncover fraudulent trends is emerging as a practical strategy [

] due to the mountain of data that investigators must go through. Therefore, Big Data analysis tools can be really useful. The most common approach to uncovering illicit financial dealings has been the use of classification strategies. Here, a dataset with class labels and feature vectors is used during the initial phase of model training. The next stage is to use the trained model to categorize test samples.

Online transactions have replaced traditional face-to-face business dealings. The banking, financial services, and insurance (BFSI) sector in India has thrived thanks to the country’s rapid economic development and the outstanding technological advances that have accompanied it. Before the advent of digital technology, human interaction was required for all business dealings and the resulting business intelligence [

]. Even though the digital revolution has made business operations more open and honest, it has also led to a deluge of data. The new discipline of analytics makes it possible to examine these digital traces in detail, leading to better business decisions [

]. The financial services sector has begun creating massive volumes of data, giving rise to a novel (BDA), as a result of an influx of youthful clients with higher expectations and broad globalization.
There has been a massive increase in the volume and type of data produced by many disciplines across science, technology, engineering, medicine, business, and economics over the past two decades [

]. This trend is being pushed even further along by the growing use of Web 2.0 social media. In order to make sense of this avalanche of information and provide a better service to customers, BDA was created. Data in the banking, finance, service, and insurance industries is generated rapidly and originates from a wide range of digital devices and forms. Big data can be summed up by its five defining features. Quantity, Velocity, Variety, Authenticity, and Value make up the five Vs. Data creation rates are measured in “volumes,” with volumes indicating the massive amounts of second. Because of the sheer number of transactions, the resulting databases are so massive that they are difficult to handle and analyze using the tools at hand. With the help of a distributed storage and parallel computational architecture, big data makes it feasible to store and process massive datasets with relative ease. Both the production and transmission rates of data are included in the velocity dimension. The term “variety” refers to the many different types of information that are generated. The big data technology handles and unifies a wide variety of data types, including text data like text messages, social media conversations, website feedbacks, tweets on Twitter; image data like Facebook, Instagram, and satellite images; data. Hence, this axis incorporates not only unstructured data, but also partially structured data, and fully structured data. The veracity axis shows how reliable the data is. This is because big data is a heterogeneous collection of organized, unstructured, and semi-structured data, making it more challenging to verify its veracity. Thus, the inherent vagueness of the data is the focus of this axis. Lastly, the Value axis characterizes the information’s monetary value [
]. It is common for the quantity of noise in the data to be much smaller than the amount of useless information. These five sizes capture the essence of big data.

The emergence of digital technology has revolutionized the banking industry. From its inception, the banking industry has shifted its focus from a singular “core banking” platform to one that supports several channels and devices. It’s vital to pay attention to both the exterior and interior of a bank. We have treasury, back-office operations, and human resources on the inside, and customers, other banks, and business partners on the outside. Considering these two factors, we may identify eight fundamental components that comprise the overall framework of a digital bank in Fig.

div.p: A circular diagram of an overall framework of digital banking involves people, customers or sales or services, regulator or other banks, internal, technology, data, B P R, and analytics.
Fig. 1
Overall framework of a digital bank

level4.p: Customer Services. Simply said, it is essential to the success of slightly digital financial system. Financial services and income generation for customers of digital banks require a holistic approach to customer relationship management (CRM). Consumers’ demands and requirements are meticulously studied before being incorporated into customer-centric business strategies.

p.span: . It includes informing the appropriate regulatory board, such as RBI, of any cases of fraud and improving internal communication across divisions. Internal. A bank’s operational risks can be evaluated and managed with the use of analytics programmed.
. Services such as core banking, internet banking, mobile banking, electronic wallets, mobile warehouse, the Data Lake, and construction all belong here. Data.
. This axis seeks to improve the firm’s productivity and earnings by rethinking and revamping the way business is conducted.

. This is vital as it increases the bank’s profitability and makes it easier to use for customers. There are three types of analytics that we identify in this context: descriptive, predictive, and prescriptive. Bar charts, line graphs, pie charts, histograms, heat maps, box plots, and other graphical representations of the data’s informational content are all examples of descriptive analytics.

. Fraud electronic or physical payment method is included here. This includes ATM, Internet, mobile, and credit/debit card fraud. The bank’s own employees are not the only potential perpetrators of fraud; customers and other outside parties are also a risk. In cases of complex cyber fraud, a worldwide ring of con artists coordinates their efforts via telephone, online forums, and email. By analyzing data from social media, text, and networks, fraud can be uncovered and stopped before it even has a chance to take hold.

. This comprises credit risk, market risk, and working risk. With the wealth of available data, many of these hazards may be quantified with statistical models, and predictions can be made using data-driven methods. Lending money to a consumer, for instance, entails a certain amount of risk that is modeled and evaluated to help guide the banking system.

level4.p: Financial big data is often collected via data centers or rented cloud incomes, which calls for vigilant control of acquisition items, channels, frequency, and target-driven personalization. Using seed sites or targets as iteratively scour the web for information using either a breadth-first or depth-first approach.

From the standpoint of structuring massive data, knowledge graphs have garnered a lot of interest since they provide a powerful and natural way to communicate knowledge. This can be thought of as a semantic network, with nodes standing in for entities or concepts and the links connecting them serving as metaphors for the many semantic linkages that can exist between them. Here, “Elon Musk” is the thing being described, and “entrepreneur” is the thing being described. “Elon Musk” “is A” “entrepreneur.” Knowledge graphs can be broken down into two distinct groups based on the kind of information sources they draw from: unstructured web pages like Google and Bing, and more organized resources like YAGO3 and DBPedia4. Together with knowledge of entities/concepts and their interactions, knowledge of events is also crucial.

Mining the value of big data is a formidable challenge after it has been meticulously organized and represented. Recently, the financial sector has paid more attention to non-traditional data sources, such as company, positioning information, and satellite images, among others, due to the wealth of information they contain on topics like market participants’ perceptions of risk. Yet, difficulties have arisen in exploring data value due to the incorporation of data from many modalities. Algorithms for processing large amounts of data have made these kinds of discoveries available to academics and financial backers.

Integrating IoT intelligence gained from the analysis of user data into various banking application flows can alert customers to potential credit card fraud. Yet, several forms of alternative data can be used in conjunction with one another to better address particular problems. Due to advancements in deep learning, a unified model may be used to combine and analyze many types of data to better identify potential financial hazards.

p.span: Below, we’ll provide a brief overview of approximately of the most common categories of financial fraud. These frauds were chosen Report (2010–2011), which was compiled for the US market.

. The term “credit card fraud” describes situations in which a person’s credit card is used to make unauthorized purchases. If the card has been lost or stolen, the transaction can still be processed by using the actual card.

. As an added bonus, they may also offer management commentary on the company’s performance and anticipated challenges. A company’s financial statements are used for many purposes, including but not limited to gauging the company’s success, influencing stock prices, and determining the company’s eligibility for factors, including a lack of awareness of the issue.

. When a policy is used fraudulently to obtain benefits from an insurer, this is known as insurance fraud. Insurance policies are typically created to safeguard the financial dealings of an enterprise or an individual against the possibility of loss

. A specific type of financial fraud, mortgage fraud relates to forgery or other manipulation of mortgage or property documentation. Misleading a lender about a property’s value to get them to loan money is a common form of fraud.

level3.p: The risk of refers to the potential harm that could come from a business or financial institution’s failure to adhere to local legislation pertaining to such crimes. Common types of financial crime include forgery of financial documents, theft of personal information, market manipulation, and money laundering. Here, we’ll discuss the new big data methods being used to oversee financial crime compliance.

p.span: By “identity theft,” we mean number or other identifying data. As a result, victims may experience a cascade of credit and other issues and sometimes suffer an abrupt loss of funds. Credit card fraud is a type of identity theft; however, it usually just affects one or a few of the victim’s active credit cards. Credit card fraud might cost as much as $ 41 billion by 2027, as indicated in Fig.

div.p: A bar graph of losses in billions versus years. The highest data is recorded in 2027 with 41 billion losses, and the lowest data recorded in 2010 with 8 billion losses. Values are estimated.
Fig. 2

Estimated global loss from payment fraud is $32.39 billion by 2020, up from$9.84 billion in 2011. The annual cost of payment fraud is estimated to reach $40.62 billion by 2027. The figures after the year 2020 are projections

level4.h4: 4.2 Financial Declaration Fraud and Insurance Fraud
level4.p: To intentionally falsify the financial condition of a business by means of the misstatement or omission of quantities or disclosures in financial reports is considered financial statement fraud. Financial statement fraud can cause massive losses for businesses. Because of this, auditors, investors, and regulators have all been quite concerned about financial statement fraud.

Money laundering refers to the practice of hiding the origin of wealth that has been illegally acquired. Two billion to $2 trillion in current amount of money laundered annually worldwide. Hence, AML is vital to a country’s economic security.
Rule-based classification is the most traditional method used by financial institutions to combat AML.

Policymakers frequently give anti-trafficking measures substantial weight. A number of technological options have been studied in recent years as potential tools in the fight against trafficking. Information gleaned from financial dealings, mobile phone calls, and text messages is extremely useful in the fight against trafficking.

level3.h3: 5 Financial Fraud Finding Challenges and Future Instructions
level3.p: The fight against financial fraud is a dynamic field where it is preferable to maintain a strategic advantage over fraudsters. It’s also clear that there are still unexplored areas of intelligent fraud detection. Here, we outline some of the major challenges in this field and offer some suggestions for future study. The following are examples of problems and obstacles that have been spotted: typical classification problems, fraud types and detection approaches, privacy considerations, computational performance, evolving problem, disproportionate misclassification costs, and generic framework.

level4.p: The banking industry’s standard operating procedures are being altered by the introduction of cutting-edge computer technology, high-end computing blockchain, and the IoT are only some of the greatest popular cutting-edge technologies currently available (IoT).

p.span: The banking system, and the financial services industry more generally, are pioneers in the adoption and use of cutting-edge technologies. For a number of years, financial institutions have relied on secluded clouds [24]. The analysis of current methods and the gaps in knowledge are shown in Table

level3.p: Insurance, banks, taxes, and businesses are just few of the areas where financial fraud can occur. Fraud involving company funds is a growing concern across all sectors of the economy. Detecting fraudulent transactions by intelligently analyzing a vast quantity of financial data is now possible with the use of machine learning-based technologies, made possible by the development of artificial intelligence. In this research Statistical and computational intelligence-based research for detecting fraud were evaluated. It was shown that each approach could detect fraud, however, results varied widely depending on the sort of fraud being looked for. SVM, two examples of excellent computational methodologies, stand out for adapt to new methods. There are still numerous unexplored aspects of brainy fraud finding. Several data mining techniques and fraud kinds have only been scratched at the surface and will require further research to fully understand detection can also be studied, and the performance of existing algorithms can be analyzed by tweaking their parameters. Finally, further in-depth analysis of the nuances between various forms of financial fraud could yield a unified framework that significantly enhances the efficacy of AI-based detection techniques.

level2.h2: Exploring the State of the Art in Mobile Agent Computing: A Comparative Study of Adaptive Fault Tolerance, Security, Transaction, and Multicore Support

p.span: Milindkumar Vaidya
Ratnadeep R. Deshmukh
and
Suhas H. Patil

div.p: Department of Computer Science and IT, Dr. Babasaheb Ambedkar Marathwada University, Aurangabad, 431004, India
Bharati Vidyapeeth University College of Engineering, Pune, 41104, India

level3.p: Milindkumar Vaidya
(Corresponding author)
Email:
Ratnadeep R. Deshmukh

level4.p: Email:

level2.p: Mobile agent computing has emerged as a promising paradigm for distributed systems, allowing intelligent software agents to migrate and execute tasks autonomously. However, the increasing complexity of these environments necessitates robust fault tolerance mechanisms, strong security measures, reliable transaction processing, and efficient utilization of multicore architectures. This paper presents a comparative study of the latest advancements in mobile agent computing, focusing on adaptive fault tolerance, security, transaction management, and multicore support. By providing insights into the strengths, weaknesses, and open research questions of existing techniques, this paper guides researchers and practitioners in designing fault-tolerant, secure, transactional, and multicore-enabled mobile agent systems. The findings contribute to the advancement of mobile agent computing by offering a comprehensive understanding of the state of the art in these key areas.

level3.p: Keywords

p.span: Mobile agent computing has gained significant attention in the field of distributed systems due to its ability to deploy intelligent software agents that can migrate and autonomously execute tasks across a network of heterogeneous devices. This paradigm offers advantages such as reduced network traffic, improved response times, and enhanced autonomy. However, as the complexity and scale of distributed environments increase, challenges related to fault tolerance, security, transaction management, and efficient utilization of multicore architectures arise [
] (Fig.

div.p: A diagram represents the architecture of the mobile host, which includes the application client and mobile agent. A wireless link is established between 3 mobile agents, which are connected to 3 application servers by a fixed network.
Fig. 1

p.span: Architecture of mobile agent computing [

level3.p: To address these challenges, this paper presents a comprehensive comparative study of the state of the art in mobile agent computing. The study focuses on four crucial aspects: adaptive fault tolerance, security, transaction management, and multicore support. By analyzing and comparing existing techniques, this research aims to provide insights into the strengths, weaknesses, and potential research directions in these areas.

p.span: Adaptive fault tolerance explores mechanisms designed to handle failures in mobile agent systems. This includes fault detection, recovery, and reconfiguration strategies, which ensure the resilience and reliability of mobile agent execution [

]. Security, examines encryption techniques, access control models, and authentication mechanisms used to protect sensitive information during agent migration and execution. By evaluating their effectiveness and vulnerabilities, this research aims to identify robust security measures for mobile agent computing [

Transaction management investigates concurrency control, atomicity, and consistency preservation techniques in the context of mobile agent systems. This study compares different transactional models to assess their performance and reliability in ensuring data integrity [

]. Multicore support explores strategies for effectively utilizing the parallel processing capabilities of multicore architectures. This includes workload distribution, load balancing, and resource optimization techniques to maximize the performance of mobile agent execution [

level3.h3: 2 Recent Techniques of Mobile Agent Computing
level3.p: Mobile agent computing is a field of research that focuses on the design and development of intelligent software agents that can migrate autonomously across distributed systems or networks. These agents can perform tasks, gather information, and interact with other agents to accomplish specific goals. In recent years, several techniques have emerged in mobile agent computing to enhance the efficiency, security, and scalability of mobile agent systems. Here are some notable techniques:

p.span: To minimize the resource overhead associated with mobile agents, lightweight agent platforms have been developed. These platforms provide a minimal execution environment for agents, reducing their memory footprint and processing requirements. This allows agents to execute efficiently on resource-constrained devices such as smartphones and Internet of Things (IoT) devices [

Mobile agents need to migrate from one host to another during their execution. Recent techniques focus on optimizing the migration process to reduce the migration time and minimize the impact on network bandwidth. Techniques such as pre-copying, post-copying, and hybrid approaches have been proposed to improve migration efficiency [

Mobile agents can benefit from context-awareness, where they gather information about their execution environment to adapt their behavior accordingly. Context-aware mobile agents can make intelligent decisions based on factors such as network conditions, device capabilities, user preferences, and location information. This enables them to dynamically adjust their execution strategy, conserve resources, and provide personalized services [

Ensuring the security and trustworthiness of mobile agent systems is crucial. Recent techniques focus on secure agent migration, authentication, and access control mechanisms. Encryption techniques are used to protect agent data during migration, while digital signatures and certificates are employed for agent authentication and authorization. Trust management models help assess the trustworthiness of agents and facilitate secure interactions among agents [

Mobile agent systems need to handle failures and ensure fault tolerance. Techniques such as checkpointing and rollback recovery mechanisms have been developed to enable agents to recover from failures and continue their execution from a known checkpoint. Additionally, redundancy and replication techniques are employed to ensure system resilience in the presence of failures [

Mobile devices often have limited battery resources, making energy efficiency a crucial consideration in mobile agent computing. Recent techniques focus on optimizing agent execution to minimize energy consumption. This includes techniques such as task offloading, dynamic voltage scaling, and adaptive agent behavior based on battery levels [

Machine learning techniques have been integrated into mobile agent computing to enhance agent intelligence and decision-making. Agents can leverage machine learning algorithms to learn from their interactions, adapt their behavior, and improve their performance over time. This integration enables agents to provide personalized services, optimize resource usage, and make intelligent decisions based on data analysis [

level4.p: These recent techniques in mobile agent computing contribute to the development of more efficient, secure, and intelligent agent systems that can operate in dynamic and distributed environments.

level3.h3: 3 Comparative Table for Adaptive Fault Tolerant in the State of Art of Mobile Agent Computing: Security, Fault Tolerance, Transaction, and Multicore Support

p.span: See Table

div.p: Table 1
Comparative table for adaptive fault tolerant in the state of art of mobile agent computing: security, fault tolerance, transaction, and multicore support

td.p: Refers to measures taken to protect mobile agents and their data from unauthorized access and malicious attacks
Authentication, access control secure code migration, trust management
Ensures that mobile agent systems can recover from failures and continue execution
Fault tolerance mechanisms for parallel execution
Deals with maintaining the consistency and reliability of transactions executed by mobile agents
Focuses on leveraging multiple cores or processors to enhance the performance of mobile agent systems
Efficient utilization of multiple cores, scalability

level3.p: Mobile agent computing has evolved over the years through continuous research and development efforts. The origins of mobile agent computing may be linked back to the early 1990s as experts began investigating the notion of mobile agents as a distributed computing solution. Here is an overview of the key stages in the development of mobile agent computing:

p.span: In the early stages, researchers focused on exploring the fundamental concepts and capabilities of mobile agents. They investigated agent mobility, migration mechanisms, and agent communication protocols. Some notable early projects include Telescript, Aglets, and Concordia [

Researchers began creating mobile agent frameworks to assist agent execution and mobility as the notion of mobile agents gained acceptance. These platforms provided a runtime environment for mobile agents, enabling them to execute on different hosts and migrate between them. Examples of early mobile agent platforms include Aglets Workbench, Grasshopper, and Voyager [

Researchers began investigating different agent migration strategies to optimize the efficiency and performance of mobile agent systems. They proposed techniques such as code mobility, state transfer, and migration control algorithms. These strategies aimed to minimize network traffic, reduce migration time, and improve fault tolerance in mobile agent systems [

As mobile agents operate in open and distributed environments, ensuring security became a critical concern. Security techniques were created by researchers to safeguard mobile agents against fraudulent domains and unauthorized access. Techniques such as secure code migration, agent authentication, access control, and secure communication protocols were developed to address security and trust management in mobile agent systems [

Mobile agent computing found practical applications in various domains. Researchers and developers started exploring the use of mobile agents in areas such as network management, e-commerce and resource discovery, distributed data mining, and distributed sensing. These applications demonstrated the potential of mobile agents in solving complex distributed computing problems [

To promote interoperability and widespread adoption of mobile agent technology, standardization efforts were initiated. The Foundation for Intelligent Physical Agents (FIPA) created a collection of standards and requirements for mobile agent systems that include topics such as communication protocols, agent management, security, and interoperability [

Researchers focused on improving the performance and efficiency of mobile agent systems. Techniques such as dynamic load balancing, adaptive migration, and optimization algorithms were developed to enhance resource utilization, reduce execution time, and optimize agent behavior in dynamic environments [

The integration of context-awareness and intelligence into mobile agent systems became a significant research area. Context-aware mobile agents can adapt their behavior based on their execution environment, user preferences, and available resources. Furthermore, machine learning techniques were incorporated to enable mobile agents to learn from their interactions, make intelligent decisions, and provide personalized services [

Middleware frameworks were developed to provide a standardized and reusable infrastructure for mobile agent systems. These frameworks offer a set of services and APIs that facilitate agent migration, communication, security, and management. Examples of mobile agent middleware include JADE (Java Agent Development Framework), Aglet SDK, and Grasshopper [

level4.p: Recent Advances: Recent developments in mobile agent computing focus on emerging technologies such as edge computing, Internet of Things (IoT), and cloud computing. Mobile agents are being utilized in edge environments to offload computation and enhance scalability. Additionally, the integration of mobile agents with cloud services enables efficient resource utilization and improved scalability.

level3.p: This paper presents a comprehensive comparative study of adaptive fault tolerance, security, transaction support, and multicore support in mobile agent computing. Through an in-depth analysis of recent advancements, we highlight the strengths, limitations, and research gaps in each area. Our study underscores the need for adaptive fault tolerance mechanisms, robust security protocols, transactional integrity, and efficient multicore utilization. This comparative analysis is a great resource for scholars and practitioners, giving perspectives on the present status of mobile agent computing and influencing future research paths to further improve the area.
